section.title: Exams
section.p:
We will have four required exams in this course. The first three are chapter exams, covering (respectively) chapters 1, 2, and 3. The final exam will cover all of chapters 14.
Chapter 1 Exam
Wednesday 10/18
Chapter 2 Exam
Monday 11/6
Chapter 3 Exam
Monday 11/27
Final Exam
Monday 12/11
For the chapter exams, we will start class that day with a review activity, typically for about one hour. Then the exam will take place. I write exams designed to take some students only 50% of the allotted time, and I would never want you to have a low score on an exam simply because there was not enough time.
The final exam will use our full two-hour time slot, and not be immediately preceded by a review session.

paragraphs.title: Alternatives
paragraphs.p:
If you cannot participate in an exam at its usual time or place, there are some alternatives. However, please note that there are no exceptions to having to take the exams with a proctor. If you need to explore the alternatives below, please contact me as soon as possible to begin discussing your options. The alternatives include:
You have an AEDR accommodation for extended time or a private working area. Then you will work with PCC AEDR at least one week in advance to schedule appointments for these exams. These might be in-person or remotely proctored, as is appropriate for your needs. You may take an exam a few days earlier than the dates listed above, but not later.
If you have a scheduling conflict, you can make an appointment with a PCC testing center for a virtually proctored exam. Email testing.remote@pcc.edu to arrange an appointment. Once it is scheduled, contact me to tell me about your appointment.
You may contact me if you have some situation that is not covered here and you would like to ask about it.

section.p: The chapter exams are each worth 11% of your final grade, and the final exam is worth 15% of your final grade.
